scope_note: QA Witt Tower Ramified Prime e=1 Period Law cert [437]. [436] proved that e=ord(lambda0 mod p) is rigid -- forced to {1,2} for det=+1 and {4} for det=-1 -- but left the e=1 case (p|(t-2) in det=+1 family) unanalysed, noting only that it is analogous to the p=2 stall in [435]. This cert analyses the e=1 case completely. When e=1, the orbit-count formula from [435] (orbit count(1)=e=1, frozen=(p+1)*p^(L-1)) breaks in two ways: fixed-point count is p (not 1=e), and frozen count is (p^2-1)*p^(L-1) (not (p+1)*p^(L-1)=(p+1)*p^(L-1)). The correct law is: period set = {p^L : L=0..k}; orbit count(1,k) = p for all k; orbit count(p^L,k=L) = (p-1)*p^(L-1) (birth); orbit count(p^L,k<L) = (p^2-1)*p^(L-1) (frozen). Mechanism: N=M-I satisfies N^2=(t-2)*M as an EXACT INTEGER identity (no approximation). When p|(t-2), N^2 ≡ 0 mod p -- N is nilpotent of degree 2 mod p. The kernel ker(N mod p) is 1-dimensional (p elements), giving count(1,k)=p for ALL k, invariant across tower levels. Primary sources: Serre, J.-P. (1979) 'Local Fields' doi:10.1007/978-1-4757-5673-9 (nilpotent lifting, ramified p-adic extensions); Ireland, K. and Rosen, M. (1990) 'A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory' ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 Ch.7 (Hensel lifting, primitive roots); Wall, D.D. (1960) American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano-period structure for quadratic recurrences). EXCEPTION (p=3 stall): for p=3 with c=(t-2)/3 ≡ 2 mod 3, a stall occurs at k=2: M^3 ≡ I mod 9 (prematurely), so no period-9 orbits form at level k=2. The formula holds at k=1 and k>=3 for stall cases; holds at all k for c≡1 mod 3. This is directly analogous to [435]'s p=2 stall. For p>=5, the formula holds universally (all c mod p, all k). SCOPE: det=+1 family only (char poly x^2-tx+1, M=[[t,-1],[1,0]], v_p(t-2)=1); p odd prime. CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): (C1) E1_PERIOD_SET: period set = {p^L:L=0..k} -- verified p=5, t=p+2..200 step p, k=1..3, zero violations PASS. (C2) E1_FIXED_COUNT: orbit count(period=1)=p for all k -- verified p=3,5,7,11, mul=1..5, k=1..4, zero failures PASS. (C3) E1_ORBIT_FORMULA: full formula verified p=5 k=1..3 and p=7 k=1..2, many t values, zero violations PASS. (C4) NILPOTENT: N^2=(t-2)*M exact integer t=3..299, zero failures; N^2≡0 mod p for all p|(t-2) PASS. (C5) P3_STALL: stall signature confirmed (no period-9 at k=2) for t=8,17,26,35; formula holds for t=5,14,23,32 at k=1..3 PASS. (C6) LEGACY: t=7/p=5, t=5/p=3, t=12/p=5 (all [436] e=1 instances) reproduce formula PASS. WHY NOT DUPLICATE [435]: [435] analyzes the e=2 (and e=4) cases and states the formula; this cert analyzes the structural difference of e=1, shows the fixed-point count changes from e to p, and explains WHY via nilpotent lifting. THEOREM NT: all arithmetic is pure integer mod p^k or exact integer identity; no float, no observer projection. 6 checks PASS; 7/7 fixtures PASS; self-test ok. Derived 2026-06-17.
